verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted disulfide-linked homodimer and heparin-binding growth factor with no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Its canonical biology is constitutive secretion into the extracellular space where it binds surface receptors (VEGFR1/2) on endothelial cells — the receptors are the surface targets, not VEGFA itself. Pre-no checklist: (1) Anti-VEGFA antibodies (bevacizumab, ranibizumab) target the circulating/soluble pool, not a cell-surface form — these are anti-soluble-ligand programs. (2) No TM precursor isoform; VEGFA is not a sheddase substrate with a stable membrane-anchored stage. (3) No documented wash-resistant covalent TM-partner tethering. (4) No documented PM minority pool; literature evidence (PMIDs cited) describes extracellular/secreted VEGFA acting on surface receptors of other cells, not VEGFA itself surfacing on the producing cell. (5) No gene-specific evidence of cancer-state ecto-display, cell-death surface exposure, germline-restricted surface display, or activation-induced PM translocation. VEGFA is secreted_only.
